title: corchorus hygrophilus
intro: Corchorus hygrophilus, also known as the dwarf jute or Indian jute, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. Corchorus hygrophilus can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried powders and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine and as dietary supplements.
